steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The blank is the main verb for the subject Maya Angelou. The phrase "along with others such as Lorenzo Thomas and Gwendolyn Brooks" is a parenthetical set off by commas — it does not change the grammatical subject. Strip it away: "The writer Maya Angelou ______ commonly associated with the Black Arts movement." The subject is singular, and the passage describes an ongoing association, so the verb must be singular present.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'have been': "have" is a plural auxiliary. Maya Angelou is one person; the subject and verb disagree. ✗
  • Choice B — 'is': singular present linking verb. "Maya Angelou is commonly associated" — subject and verb agree in number and tense. ✓
  • Choice C — 'are': plural present. Same number error as A. ✗
  • Choice D — 'were': plural past. Wrong in both number and tense; the passage describes an ongoing association, not a past one. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
